summary: Kenneth Suna discusses mindset shifts for beginning investors, emphasizing starting with small amounts ($5/month) in low-cost ETFs like S&P 500 Index Funds to build an investor mentality rather than a consumer mentality. The focus is on psychological habits and gradual capital accumulation through lifestyle choices, not trading strategies.
takeaways: Start with $5/month minimum in a low-cost S&P 500 Index ETF
The goal is the mindset shift from consumer to investor, not immediate returns
Use decision moments (e.g., discretionary purchases) to redirect spending toward investments instead
Incrementally increase investment amounts month-over-month through small lifestyle cuts
Avoid overcomplicating the process and reject get-rich-quick courses
title: Start small #shorts
